title: Deaths from road injuries
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.
caption:
Deaths from road injuries

Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.

This choropleth world map displays the annual number of deaths from road injuries in 2023. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for zero deaths to dark red for 100,000 deaths, with intermediate bands at 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, and 30,000. The darkest red shade, indicating values at or above 100,000, is visible in China and India. The United States, Brazil, and Russia appear in a dark orange-red band that corresponds to the 30,000 to 100,000 range. A broad cluster of countries, including Mexico, Indonesia, Iran, and several nations in Eastern Europe and South America, sits in the mid-range orange bands between 3,000 and 30,000. Much of Western Europe, Canada, and Australia are depicted in lighter shades of orange and beige, placing them in the lower bands between 0 and 3,000. Greenland and a few small island territories are marked with a hatched pattern, signifying no data. The visual distribution shows the highest absolute numbers of road injury deaths concentrated in the most populous nations of Asia and the Americas, while many countries in Europe and Oceania occupy the lower end of the scale.
